raw_text:
Nexus Labs // Junior Android Engineer

San Francisco, CA (Remote-Friendly) | Full-Time | $80,000 - $110,000 per year

Building intuitive mobile experiences that connect communities

>> ABOUT

Nexus Labs is a growth-stage mobile-first technology company founded in 2019,
specializing in consumer social applications with a focus on privacy-first design

principles. We serve over 2 million active users globally and are committed to creating
best-in-class Android experiences. Our engineering team values collaboration, continuous

learning, and shipping quality features on a regular cadence.

>> SUMMARY

Join Nexus Labs as a Junior Android Engineer and help us build the next generation of

mobile social applications. You'll work on our flagship Android app, collaborating
closely with senior engineers and product teams to develop new features, optimize

performance, and ensure exceptional user experiences. This is an ideal role for a
motivated early-career engineer eager to master modern Android development using Kotlin

and Jetpack libraries.

>> RESPONSIBILITIES

- Develop new features and user interfaces using Kotlin and Jetpack Compose following

established design systems and architectural patterns
- Implement reactive asynchronous operations using Coroutines to ensure smooth,

responsive user interactions
- Design and implement local data persistence layers using Room database framework

with proper migration strategies
- Write unit and integration tests using JUnit and Espresso to ensure code quality and

prevent regressions
- Participate in code reviews with senior engineers to learn best practices and

provide constructive feedback to peers
- Troubleshoot and debug issues in the app using Android Studio, Logcat, and network

monitoring tools to rapidly identify root causes
- Collaborate with designers and product managers to translate wireframes and

specifications into functional Android features
- Contribute to the app's Play Store releases, including version management, release

notes, and monitoring app performance metrics post-launch

>> MUST HAVE

- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related field (or

equivalent professional experience)
- 1-2 years of professional Android development experience with shipped production

applications
- Strong proficiency in Kotlin programming language and Object-Oriented design

principles
- Solid understanding of Android lifecycle, Activities, Fragments, and Services

- Experience with version control systems (Git) and familiarity with collaborative
development workflows

- Ability to write clear, maintainable code with attention to performance and memory
optimization

 >> NICE TO HAVE

- Experience with Jetpack Compose or modern declarative UI frameworks

- Familiarity with Coroutines and reactive programming patterns
- Prior experience with REST APIs and JSON parsing libraries

- Understanding of Material Design principles and responsive UI patterns

>> STACK

Kotlin / Jetpack Compose / Android Coroutines / Room Database / Android Studio / JUnit &

Espresso / Retrofit & OkHttp / Android Jetpack (ViewModel, LiveData, Navigation) / Play Store

Console / Firebase Analytics

>> BENEFITS

- Competitive health, dental, and vision insurance (100% of premiums covered for

employee)
- Generous paid time off: 20 days annual leave + 10 company holidays

- Professional development budget of $2,000 annually for conferences, courses, and
certifications

- Collaborative home office setup stipend ($500) and ongoing remote work equipment
support

- Free lunch and beverage programs at our San Francisco office for in-office days
- Mentorship program pairing junior engineers with senior staff for career growth

- 401(k) matching up to 4% of salary contributions

>> APPLY

Submit your resume, a portfolio link (GitHub, personal website, or Google Play store

apps), and a cover letter explaining your interest in Android development to
careers@nexuslabs.io. Include links to any open-source contributions or sample projects.

We review applications on a rolling basis and typically respond within 1-2 weeks.
